rulings.Case Title: FAHIMA ZAHIR VS. 18 LANSING STREET OWNERS'S ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday January 16, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFF FAHIMA ZAHIR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion is denied to the extent Plaintiff is seeking to allege a cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty against the Association (as opposed to directors). Demurrer to that cause of action had been previously sustained without leave to amend. (November 16, 2018 Order). Third Amended Complaint to be filed and served forthwith. The Court makes no rulings on the sufficiency and or propriety of allegations made in the proposed Third Amended Complaint at this time. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER MORGAN ET AL VS. SYDA DAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings By Defendants Syda Day, Meteorite Real Estate, Llc And Vithal, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday January 16, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT SYDNEY DAY, VITHAL, LLC, METEORITE, LLC Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings By Defendants Syda Day, Meteorite Real Estate, Llc And Vithal, Llc GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to the fourth cause of action for violation of SFRO 37.10B as Defendant is not a "landlord" within the meaning of SFRO and Plaintiffs are not "tenants." Motion (based on preemption) is DENIED as to the second and fourth causes of action. While FEHA, SF Police Code and SFRO employ similar regulatory tools to proscribe discrimination, they do not occupy the same "field." Specifically, FEHA's purpose is to protect civil rights as opposed to the "subject matter" of SF Police Code and SFRO. See SF Police Code, Article 1.2, Section 100 and SFRO 37.1(a). = (501/CFH)
